title: EGFR alterations and targeted therapy response in lung cancer
abstract: In this toy abstract, EGFR activating variants are associated with response to tyrosine kinase inhibitors in non-small cell lung cancer. Acquired resistance is described after first-line inhibitor exposure, and follow-up testing is needed to identify resistance-associated alterations.

title: BRAF V600E as a biomarker in melanoma treatment
abstract: This sample record describes BRAF V600E melanoma as a setting where targeted inhibitors can produce tumor responses. Combination strategies are discussed as a way to delay pathway reactivation. The abstract is synthetic and intended for retrieval testing only.

title: Neoantigen prediction from tumor exome and RNA profiles
abstract: This toy study outlines a neoantigen prediction workflow that combines somatic variant calls, HLA typing, RNA expression, and peptide binding scores. Candidate ranking depends on expression support and predicted antigen presentation, and results require experimental validation.

title: TP53 mutation status and tumor biology
abstract: This synthetic abstract discusses TP53 mutation status as a broad marker of altered tumor suppressor biology. It notes that TP53 status can correlate with prognosis in some cohorts, but interpretation depends on cancer type, assay design, and study context.

title: Single-cell evidence synthesis for target discovery
abstract: This sample abstract describes a target discovery support workflow using single-cell expression, pathway annotations, and literature evidence. IL6 and TNF are used as example inflammatory signals, and the workflow prioritizes hypotheses rather than confirmed clinical targets.

title: Conflicting toy evidence for a biomarker claim
abstract: This synthetic record describes a small cohort where BRAF status was not associated with durable response to targeted inhibitor treatment in melanoma. The result is presented as conflicting toy evidence and would require manual review in a real literature workflow.

title: Synthetic potency comparison of EGFR inhibitors
abstract: In this synthetic in vitro assay, osimertinib inhibited mutant EGFR with an IC50 of 12 nM, whereas gefitinib inhibited the same target with an IC50 of 38 nM. The osimertinib plasma half-life was approximately 48 h in the toy dataset.

title: Toy receptor pharmacology of a beta-2 agonist
abstract: This toy pharmacology record reports that salbutamol activated the beta-2 adrenergic receptor with an EC50 of 3.2 nM and a Ki of 1.1 nM. Reported oral bioavailability was about 50% in the synthetic example.
